entry.title: Cannot restore SAP HANA Database using lob backups
entry.content:
Is it possible restore an SAP PERSON_NAME using only Log backups?


--- Steps to Reproduce ---

With SNAP Centes:

  1. Restore SNAP
  2. Restore Log backups to a local File System
  3. Restore PITR point in time recovery using log backups

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Cannot restore SAP HANA Database using lob backups
entry.content:
Dear customer,

Thank you for contacting SAP Support.

To give the conclusion first, it's not possible to perform the restore with log backups only.
It's because log backups are dependent on the specific data backups which has been taken previously.
Regarding to your concern, would you please refer to KBA 3556564 - Are log backups required while recovering point in time sap HANA using incremental backups ? 
It's not the exactly same situation as this case.
However, you can have the answer from the statement below in CAUSE section.

"If you wish to recover SAP HANA using differential or incremental backups, you must also use log backups. If log backups are not available, you can only recover using a full data backup."

So, you should prepare the corresponding data backup as well as log backups.
You can check which data and log backups are required for the needed point in time recovery by running the output of hdbbackupdiag OS command.
For details about hdbbackupdiag, please refer to Notes below.

1821207 - Determining required recovery files 
1873247 - Checking recoverability with hdbbackupdiag --check.  

As for how to recover HANA database using a data snapshot, please refer to 'Recover SAP HANA From a Data Snapshot' in HANA Admin guide.

Best regards,
SAP Support

entry.title: Re: Installation SAP Data Services 4.3
entry.content:
Hi @rouchad_abderrazak1042 , below you can find an answer for your questions:

CMS (Central Management Server) is the core service that manages BI/IPS information. It'll handle user authentication, security, permissions and Object Metadata.

CMS is not a Standalone product, for example, you'll not find a "CMS installer" on SAP Support Portal. Instead, CMS is a component which comes bundled with BI/IPS Installation.
Data Services does not have its own user management, so it uses the BI/IPS as its security engine.

CMS is part of BI/IPS, not Data Services. We have a mandatory Installation Sequence:

  1. First, you'll install BI/IPS, since Data Services will run on top of it. At first, you must have a database ready to hold the CMS system database. During the IPS installation, CMS will be created.
  2. Data Services will be installed later, on top of BI/IPS. Also, during the Data Services Installation, the installer will ask you to enter CMS credentials you created in the first step.

It is a mandatory step-by-step, even if you do not have intention to use BI launchpad. Data Services will require CMS to work.
You'll use the CMC (Central Management Console), which is the "web face" of CMS, to register your repositories so SAP Data Services Designer can find them.

You can find more information in the documentation below:

  • Adding a CMS connection | SAP Help Portal
  • Specify CMS | SAP Help Portal
  • Central Management Server (CMS) | SAP Help Portal

Also, if you'd like to understand more about Data Services, you can check the product help portal below. You can search for any component you'd like to better understand there 🙂

  • SAP Data Services | SAP Help Portal | SAP Help Portal

Best Regards,
Júlia
Data Services Product Support

entry.title: SLT System Migration – SQL Server on Windows 2019 to SAP HANA on RHEL | Best Approach?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I'm working on a system migration for our SAP Landscape Transformation (SLT) environment and would appreciate guidance from those who've done something similar.

Current Setup:

  • SLT source DB: Microsoft SQL Server running on Windows Server 2019

Target Setup:

  • Database: SAP HANA
  • OS: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL)

Background: This migration is part of a broader initiative — once the SLT system is on HANA, it will be used to support a BDC (Business Data Cloud) scenario downstream.

My Question: What is the recommended approach for this kind of heterogeneous system migration (OS change + DB change)?

Specifically:

  • Can SUM (Software Update Manager) handle this type of migration (DB + OS switch)?
  • Is SWPM (Software Provisioning Manager) the right tool here?
  • Or is there a completely different recommended path (e.g., System Copy + DB migration using HANA MDC)?

Any pointers to relevant SAP Notes, guides, or real-world experience would be greatly appreciated!

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: #COE#RFL01 Question about special tax code for Belgiu...
entry.content:
Hi Teddy,

Please check following option of Custom Tax Procedure Modification,

Configuration Steps:

  1. Copy and Modify Tax Procedure (OBCO)

    • Copy the standard Belgium procedure (TAXB/TAXBEL)
    • Create a new condition type (e.g., ZNVV) for non-deductible credit-side VAT
    • Assign a new account key (e.g., ZNV) to this condition type
    • Set the calculation base to the non-deductible percentage
    • Ensure the condition posts to the credit side

  2. Define Account Key (OB40)

    • Create account key ZNV
    • Assign it to a VAT clearing account (credit side)
    • Ensure the account has tax category ">" (input tax) or "<" (output tax) as appropriate

  3. Configure Tax Code (FTXP)

    • Create a new tax code (e.g., Z1)
    • Assign percentages:
      • VST: Deductible percentage (e.g., 10.5% if 50% of 21% is deductible)
      • NVV: Non-deductible percentage (e.g., 10.5% for debit side)
      • ZNVV: Non-deductible percentage (e.g., 10.5% for credit side to VAT account)
      • ESA/ESE: Full VAT percentage (e.g., 21% for reverse charge credit)

  4. Assign GL Accounts (OB40)

    • VST: Input VAT account (debit)
    • NVV: Expense account (debit)
    • ZNV: VAT clearing account (credit)
    • ESA/ESE: Output VAT account (credit)

Posting Logic:

  • Debit side:
    • Expense account: Invoice amount
    • Input VAT account (VST): Deductible VAT
    • Expense account (NVV): Non-deductible VAT
  • Credit side:
    • Supplier account: Invoice amount only (not including non-deductible VAT)
    • Output VAT account (ESA/ESE): Full VAT amount (reverse charge)
    • VAT clearing account (ZNV): Non-deductible VAT (offsetting credit)

Pros/Cons:

  • Advantages: Clean, automated solution; maintains audit trail; integrates with VAT reporting
  • Limitations: Requires modification of tax procedure (may need SAP Note or custom development); potential impact on system upgrades

If the custom tax procedure approach is not feasible due to system constraints or SAP support limitations:

  1. Manual Journal Entry Process:

    • Post the invoice with a standard co-contractor tax code
    • Manually post a correcting entry to move the non-deductible credit from vendor to VAT clearing account
    • Document the process in a standard operating procedure

  2. Periodic Adjustment Postings:

    • Accumulate non-deductible credits during the month
    • Post a single adjustment at month-end to reclassify from vendor to VAT clearing
    • Reconcile vendor accounts to ensure accuracy

  3. Custom Report for Tracking:

    • Develop a custom report to identify invoices with tax code Z1
    • Generate a list of required adjustments
    • Use the report to guide manual postings

Please note, this configuration requires careful testing and validation. The credit-side routing of non-deductible VAT to a tax account instead of the vendor is non-standard and may require custom development or an SAP consulting engagement. Always involve your tax advisor and SAP technical team before implementing.

entry.title: Re: CKMLQS wrong quantity structure caused by wrong by-pr...
entry.content:
In Material Ledger (CKMLQS), the system derives the actual BOM from all procurement alternatives that were active during the period. Even though you reversed the posting of SFG2 as a by‑product, the system still considers that procurement alternative as part of the BOM structure. Because SFG2 had input materials (700 raw), the ledger is “rolling up” those costs into FG, even though the output quantity of SFG2 is effectively zero for FG consumption.

Having said that, yes you are correct. Logically, zero output should mean zero scaled input. SAP’s “standard behavior” explanation is technically true, but it’s a limitation of CKMLQS reporting rather than a reflection of actual consumption. In practice, the costs are not wrongly allocated; it is the display that is misleading.

If this is critical for audit or reporting, the workaround is usually to rely on CKM3N or custom reporting, because CKMLQS doesn’t handle reversed alternatives gracefully.

entry.title: Re: MM LPA Scheduling Agreement and metal product pricing
entry.content:
Why you are facing this issue is because, LPA Scheduling Agreements use time-dependent conditions (info records or contracts) to determine the GR price. Supplier invoices reflect daily market prices, which often differ from the GR-date condition. This results into Invoice verification flags discrepancies, leading to blocked invoices.

Common approaches which you can consider

1. Material Ledger / Actual Costing
Capture daily market price differences in price differences accounts. At period-end, Material Ledger settlement redistributes variances to inventory and COGS. The benefit due to this is it keeps GR consistent while reflecting actual invoice prices later.

2. Condition Maintenance Automation
Automate daily condition updates in the scheduling agreement (via IDoc, BAPI, or custom interface). Ensures GR price matches invoice price more closely. But the challenge is it requires integration with market price feeds or supplier systems.

3. Tolerance Keys in MIRO
Configure tolerance limits for invoice verification (transaction OMR6). Allows small price differences to pass without blocking. This is best for minor fluctuations, not large daily swings.

4. Alternative Pricing Logic
Some customers use user exits (e.g., EXIT_SAPLMRMC_001) or BAdIs (MRM_HEADER_CHECK) to override invoice price checks. Can accept supplier invoice price as valid even if it differs from GR condition. But the risk is custom logic must be carefully controlled to avoid compliance issues.

5. Split Procurement Strategy
Use LPA Scheduling Agreements for logistics/JIT delivery planning. Use Contracts or POs for price determination when market volatility is high.

entry.title: SAP JCO : Limiting output of RFC SAPTUNE_GET_SUMMARY_STATISTICS to a specific table
entry.content:
Hi all,

Is there a way to restrict the output of the RFC SAPTUNE_GET_SUMMARY_STATISTICS to retrieve data for only a specific table, such as BUFFER_STATISTICS?

Currently, when we execute this RFC via SAP JCo, it returns the entire dataset, which is quite large. We are looking for a way to fetch only the required subset (e.g., a single table) to improve performance and reduce data volume.

Is there any parameter, filter, or alternative RFC that allows querying specific statistics instead of the full output?

Any suggestions or best practices would be helpful.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: 【保存版】Joule for Consultants (J4C) セルフサービス導入・完全ステップガイド
entry.content:
Joule for Consultants（以下 J4C）は、コンサルタントだけでなく、営業やプリセールスの生産性をも劇的に変える強力なツールです。

J4Cの導入を検討されている皆様についに朗報です！2026年に入り、待望の「SAP Storeでの直接購入」が可能になりました。

営業担当（AE）とのやり取りを待たず、セルフサービスでスピーディに導入できるようになった一方で、設定を一つ間違えると「契約したのに3ヶ月間も使えない」という深刻なトラブルに繋がるケースも……。

本記事では、最短ルートで導入するための具体的な手順と、実務担当者が絶対に踏んではいけない「落とし穴」を詳しく解説します。

まず、「ストア購入」vs「営業担当（AE）経由」どっちが向いている？

「セルフサービス（SAP Store）」と「営業担当（AE）経由」、どちらのルートで進めるべきか迷われるかもしれません。
結論から言うと、「まずは少人数で、今すぐ試したい」ならSAP Store、「全社導入でコストを最適化したい」ならAE経由がおすすめです。

具体的な違いを以下の表にまとめました。

項目SAP Store (セルフ)SAP AE (営業担当) 経由
手続きスピード最短当日（セルフ）数日〜数週間（調整による）
契約期間1年〜選択可能通常3年〜
価格交渉不可(定価販売)大規模導入の場合には割引の可能性あり
向いている人まず数名で試したい、急ぎ大規模導入、長期利用を予定

ステップ1：SAP Storeでの購入（AI Unitの契約）

まず、J4Cを利用するためのライセンス枠である SAP AI Units (SKU: 8019164) を契約します。

導入前に：J4Cの「数量計算」と「コスト」の考え方

J4Cはセルフサービスの購入では1ユーザーから、大きな初期投資なしで始められるのが最大の魅力です。

  • 消費の目安: 1ユーザーあたり 月間 35 AI Units

  • 最小購入単位: 100 AI Units（ ＝ 1ブロック ＝ リスト価格 約700ユーロ〜）

  • 10名で1年間使う場合の計算例: 10名 × 12ヶ月 × 35 Units ＝ 4,200 Units
    👉SAP Store等では 「42ブロック」 を購入することになります。

  • 柔軟な運用: 月単位で、利用ユーザーの変更や増減が可能です。

    • 例：4,200 Units購入し、最初の2ヶ月だけ30名で集中利用（2,100 Units消費）して有用性を判断する、といった使い方も可能です。

💡「10名以上」のススメ

J4Cは特定の個人に割り当てる「バイネーム形式」のライセンスです。アカウントの使い回し（シェア）は明確なライセンス違反となります。 組織として「本当に効果があるか？」を正しく判断するためには、コンサル、開発、PM、営業など複数のロールで活用を比較できるよう、まずは10名以上での検証を強く推奨しています。

購入の条件

以下の条件を満たす場合のみ、SAP Storeから直接購入可能です。

  • 過去に旧AI Unitを購入したことがない

  • 購入履歴が SKU 8019164（最新のAI Unit）のみ

  • （※TDD版のAI Unitの購入履歴については場合は全く問題ありません）

該当する場合は、SAP Storeで 「Buy as a customer」 を選択して購入してください。
これら以外の場合は、担当のAE（営業）を通した購入が必要です。

⚠️購入時の最重要チェックポイント：Customer ID (BPID)

購入プロセスで「どのCustomer IDに対して契約するか」を選択します。

これは「契約の主体」を決める作業です。 多くのパートナー企業様は「PartnerとしてのID」と「CustomerとしてのID」の二つを持っている可能性があり、どちらにライセンスを紐づけるかで、後の管理画面（SAP for Me）での見え方が変わります。 ここで間違ったIDを選んでしまうと、既存のS-Userでログインできず、有効化依頼（Case作成）ができないトラブルが発生します。

「解決に3ヶ月かかった」事例もあるため、必ず事前に社内で議論してください。

ステップ2：BTPアカウントの紐付け（基盤の準備）

購入プロセスの中で、J4Cをプロビジョニング（セットアップ）したい場所（SAP BTP Global Account）を指定します。

  • 既存アカウントがある場合: リストから適切な Global Account を選択します。部門専用の限定的なものではなく、中央管理用の共通アカウントが推奨されます。

    • ポイント: 将来的に Custom Joule Agent を独自開発する場合もAI Unitが必要です。SAP Build（開発ツール）を利用している既存のBTP Global Account があれば、そこを選択するのが最も効率的です。 

      ⚠️注意：BTP Global Account IDの確認

      BTPのGlobal Accountも会社の中で用途が違うものが複数ある可能性が高く、Joule で利用するBTP Global Accountを正しく指定する必要があります。ステップ1が「契約」なら、こちらは「設置場所」の指定です。
      前述の通り、J4Cのプロビジョニング先のBTP Global Accountを間違えると、ライセンスが届いているはずなのに「自分のBTP画面に表示されない」といったトラブルになり、解消に時間がかかります。

      必ず「J4CをデプロイしたいアカウントのID」を事前に確認してください。

       

  • 新規作成（Create New）の場合: 選択すると、SAPが無料で新しいBTPアカウントを発行します。数分〜数時間以内にログイン情報が届きます。

ステップ3：Discovery Centerでの有効化（テクニカルセットアップ）

購入完了後、実際の機能を有効化する作業が必要です。

  1. 公式ミッションの利用: SAP Discovery Center: Enable SAP Joule for Consultants にアクセスし、詳細な手順を確認してください。

  2. 主な設定作業:

    • サービスの有効化: BTPコントロールパネルから「Joule for Consultants」サービスを有効化。

    • ロールの割り当て: 利用者に Joule_Consultant_User 等のロールコレクションを割り当てます。

    • 認証設定: 必要に応じて SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) との連携を確認します。

最後に

「コンサル限定」から「全社的なSAPチャットボット」へ

J4Cは当初、技術者や機能コンサルタントを主なターゲットとしていました。しかし現在、活用シーンは劇的に広がっています。

  • プリセールス・営業: 顧客への提案資料のドラフト作成や、問い合わせへの回答アイデア出しに活用。

  • サポート部門: 複雑なSAP仕様の確認スピード向上。

あるパートナー企業では、「SAPビジネスに関わる全員」にJ4Cを配布したそうです。もはや技術者専用のツールではなく、「SAPに関するあらゆる疑問に答えてくれる社内専用AIアシスタント」として、営業からサポートまで幅広く活用するのがこれからのスタンダードになりそうです。

J4Cは「小さく始められ、月単位で柔軟に運用できる」非常に強力な投資対象です。2026年からは Development Fund (DF) の活用も可能になっています（※購入前のPBM相談が必須です！）。

組織としての導入メリットを正しく判断するためにも、まずは10ユーザー程度で、営業・コンサル・開発が一体となった検証からスタートしてみませんか？

詳細のJ4C検討ガイドについてはこちらをご覧ください。

entry.title: Re: Accounting Clerk - Dunning
entry.content:
Good day @sanne_larsen 

Thank you for your question.

The short answer to your scenario is simply, No, you cannot achieve the multi person team assignment. Here is why:

In Public Cloud, the Accounting Clerk in the BP company code data is designed as a single value. Hence, the configuration for defining Accounting Clerks using one identification code and one user ID per clerk, and that clerk code is then maintained in the BP’s company code data.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/2949c65446049021e10000000a44176d.html?locale=en-US

Because of this design, there is no standard option to assign several accounting clerks to one BP or to create several clerks with the same ID.

I suggest you create one generic accounting clerk ID for the AR team and use a shared mailbox for the correspondence email. So, I recommend you set it up this way:

  • Create one clerk ID that represents the AR team
  • Assign that clerk ID to the BP
  • Maintain a shared team email address for correspondence and dunning communication.

This, then serves as a team based operational model within the standard single clerk design.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/e616af23d2b947fbb275deb759b7090b.html?locale=en-US

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Increasing Sales Performance: Why aren’t we talking about the cognitive load of sales travel?
entry.content:

entry.title: Re: How to Derive Serial Number from Inventory Goods Issu...
entry.content:
Asset-Equipment synchronization is the delivered integration, but since you don’t want to use it, you will need a workaround and below are some workaround options.

1) Business Process Automation (BTP / RAP Extension)
Use a custom logic extension (via Business Add-In / In-App extensibility) triggered at the time of asset creation or goods issue. Read the serial number from the material document (MIGO / goods issue posting). Write it into the Asset Master’s serial number field automatically.

2) Integration via APIs
SAP S/4HANA Cloud provides APIs for both Material Documents and Asset Master Data. You can build a small integration (e.g., on SAP BTP) that listens for goods issue events, extracts the serial number, and updates the asset master.

3) Custom CDS View + Workflow
Create a CDS view that joins material documents (movement type 241) with asset postings. Use a workflow or RPA bot to populate the serial number field in the asset master.

entry.title: Dynamic Display of Dimension Properties in Table using Checkbox (SAC)
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,
 
I have a requirement in SAP Analytics Cloud and would appreciate your guidance.
 
I have a table with the following dimensions:
• Profit Center (general dimension)
• GL Account (Account Dimension)
• Partner Unit (general dimension)
 
The Profit Center dimension has around 25 properties (for example: Brand, Location, etc.).
 
Requirement:
 
I want to provide users with a checkbox (or multi-selection option) where:
• If the user selects certain properties (e.g., Brand, Location), those properties should be displayed in the table.
• If the user unchecks them, the properties should be removed from the table dynamically.
 
Question:
 
How can this be achieved using JavaScript in SAC (Analytics Designer)?
Is there a way to dynamically add/remove dimension properties as columns based on user selection?
 
Any suggestions or sample approaches would be really helpful.
 
Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Re: I_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEMTEXTTP is empty in the LongText...
entry.content:
Good day @david_lopez_celis 

Thank you for your question.

If I_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEMTEXTTP returns an empty LongText, then you probably did not maintain any billing item long text for that billing document item. If that is the case, the CDS will not provide a meaningful long text value. The screenshot below is an example of a scenario where no billing item text was maintained.

If your goal is to retrieve a similar text value for reporting or integration, then the first standard alternative to check is I_BillingDocumentItemBasic. That CDS contains BillingDocumenItemText and it usually the correct source for the regular billing item text.

In your tenant:

  • Pick one billing document item where I_BILLINGDOCUMENTITEMTEXTTP shows an empty LongText.
  • Check whether a billing item text was actually maintained for that item.
  • In parallel, read the same item from I_BillingDocumentItemBasic.
  • Then compare LongText from the text CDS and BillingDocumentItemText from I_BillingDocumentItemBasic.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/03c04db2a7434731b7fe21dca77440da/e1c01ea7b6aa48a8a78778a523f5902b.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/03c04db2a7434731b7fe21dca77440da/4b251ad50414425885c0318fca92307a.html?locale=en-US

Data Browser was filtered for billing document to verify whether BillinDocumentItemText is maintained for the selected billing item. BillinDocumentItemText  can be used as the standard alternative when LongText is empty

Based on a quick test carried out for your scenario, the best standard alternative is to use BillinDocumentItemText from the released billing item CDS, for example I_BillingDocumentItemItem or I_BillingDocumentItemBasic, depending on what is released in your tenant.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: Copy Control Routine 1
entry.content:
When you create a Sales Order with reference to a Contract, the system uses copy control rules to decide which partner functions are transferred. By default, the Bill-To and Payer are copied from the Contract header, not re-derived from the Customer Master partner functions of AA/AW.

If you want to have different Partner Function in sales order, in VOPAN, you have a field "Source" (in your screen shot, it is blank) where you can assign AA & AW under this tab but even for Contract, system will consider the same. If the requirement is to have only in sales order and not in preceding document, you can use a BAdI (e.g., SD_PARTNER_DETERMINE) to replace those two fields at Sales Order creation.

entry.title: Re: IEM: custom alerts filters and Snow ticket creation
entry.content:
Hello Customer,

How did you make changes to the filters in 'Integration & Exception Monitoring' configuration? 
There are "Event Filters" and "Data Collection filters" that can be found in Cloud ALM Expert Portal.
In some cases, data collection filter doesn't work as expected. 
For example,  2 filters,  Filter1: USER NE <ABC>, Filter2: USER NE <BCD>  are configured for the same category.  
It is expected to collect data with user not equal to ABC and user not equal to BCD. 
However, the data with all the users (including ABC and BCD) was collected.

That is because setting up a filter to exclude 2 values for one attribute is not supported yet. For example: USER NE (is not) ABC, BCD.
This is a known limitation. SAP Development team is working on a revamping of the configuration UI and plans to release those improvements in 2026.

You may refer to the following relative KBAs to get a general picture about how to maintian filters.

3655564 - Data collection filter does not work as expected in SAP Cloud ALM Exception Monitoring
3594515 - Filtering with wildcards (*) does not work in Integration & Exception Monitoring

Please let us know how is the filter configured.
I will be assisting you based on the current settings.

Best regards,
Ellie

entry.title: Re: ME23N: PO Expected to Trigger Message Type NEUG but S...
entry.content:
To your question "Where to set Breakpoints", please follow the below steps

Function Modules:

  • ME_OUTPUT_DETERMINATION → main driver for PO output determination.
  • NAST_PROTOCOL_UPDATE → updates message determination logs.
  • RV_MESSAGES_UPDATE → generic routine for message determination.

Includes:

  • MM06EF0OUTPUT → output determination logic for purchasing.
  • MM06EF0NAST → handles NAST table updates.
  • Set breakpoints here to trace how the system decides between NEU and NEUG.

Verify the access sequence fields (e.g., document type, vendor, purchasing org). If NEU and NEUG share the same access sequence, the system will pick the first valid condition record it finds. Check the priority/order of condition records in transaction NACR. Ensure NEUG’s condition record is not being skipped due to missing field values (like vendor or purchasing org mismatch).

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh + AMQP: The Retry Trap Nobody Warns You About
entry.content:
@RubikWal  to reprocess a single message parked in Dead Letter Queue in CPI it needs to be moved to a respective original JVM queue where it came from. Usually that is a manual operation.

So in the master data example you provided, what will happen is:

  1. iFlow that fails because of MD issues will undergo several retries by JMS built in mechanism 
  2. When the set retries number is reached, the respective message will be parked in Dead Letter Queue by JMS build it logic and stay there until manual intervention
  3. Once MD issue is fixed we can get back to the failed iFlow and move it from DLQ to the original one manually
  4. To do so we go to DLQ, find the message by it's message ID and move it to the original JMS queue
  5. The message gets picked and processed by the same iFlow that used to fail due to DM issue (hopefully successfully this time)
  6. Profit

entry.title: Re: How to enforce a Maximum Stock Level per MRP Area (IT...
entry.content:
In S/4HANA, there is no standard way to enforce a “hard stop” on replenishment at the MRP Area level using the Maximum Stock field. MRP always covers demand. The recommended approaches are either using a new BAdI to filter MRP proposals or shifting the capacity control to EWM

Below would be the recommended steps

  • Define the maximum stock threshold at MRP Area level (custom Z-table or enhancement).
  • Implement BAdI PPH_SUPPLY_DEMAND_LIST to suppress proposals once stock ≥ threshold.
  • Enhance ZMM_DISPATCH to check stock before STO creation.
  • If EWM is in scope, shift the constraint logic to EWM capacity checks for a more robust solution.
  • Document the logic clearly, since standard SAP does not provide a “hard stop” in MRP.

entry.title: Re: Delete created defect templates in SAP Cloud ALM?
entry.content:
Hello Customer,

There is a way to set the template defect as obsolete or remove it permanently.

1. In defects page, display the template defect that you want to delet, click on the "..." menu associated with this template and select the "set to obsolete" option.
2. Navigate to the filter settings and add the state.
3. Select state "obsolete" to filter out the template.
4. Once the template is displayed, select it and click on the "Delete" button to remove it.

The screen shots provided in KBA 3699863 are for your reference.

Best regards,
Ellie

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2603 – What’s New?
entry.content:
Get ready for a more powerful and flexible logistics experience. We’ve just released significant updates to SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and Global Track and Trace, designed to improve your efficiency and visibility.

Now you can:

  • Collaborate with carriers in new and better ways.
  • Track shipments with an enhanced, streamlined process.
  • Gain deeper insights when transporting dangerous goods.
  • Manage logistics across an even wider range of transport modes.

Here are the highlights at a glance.

Cross SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

New learning content: BN200 learning journey ‘Implementing SAP Business Network for Logistics’ released

Learning Journey BN200 was newly released! It is targeted at customers and partners to develop their skills on ‘Implementing SAP Business Network for Logistics’. The course is free of charge and takes about three hours to complete.

The journey’s goals are:

  • Onboard Shipper and Carrier tenants.
  • Configure SAP BTP and provision SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace solutions.
  • Integrate SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace with SAP Transportation Management.
  • Monitor and troubleshoot integration issues.
  • Configure the system with business partner data and set it up for location data replication and scenarios such as subcontracting, tendering, dock appointment scheduling, milestone reporting, and settlement.
  • Model management and customization in SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace.

Process enhancement event tracking: Consumer API for Order Tracking (FC and GTT) and Provider API for Order Tracking (FC and GTT) now support new event types and event reason code

In the Consumer API for Order Tracking (FC and GTT) and Provider API for Order Events (FC and GTT), there are three new event types:

  • CHECK_IN (Check-In)
  • SUB_VEHICLE_READY (Subsequent Vehicle Ready)
  • GATEIN_HOLD (Gate Check-In on Hold)

In the GTT ’Track Shipments’ app, you can view these event types in the tracking timeline and ’Events’ table. In the ’Manage Freight Execution’ app, you can also report these event types. Please note: These events can't be synchronized back to TM in SAP S/4HANA.

In the code list documentation, the code list Event Code Descriptions is enhanced to include the new event type codes.

The following new event reason code is now supported:

  • BAD_ORDER (Bad Order)

The code list Event Reason Code Descriptions was enhanced.

 

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Improved data quality for dangerous goods: Consumer API for Freight Subcontracting: new field added for dangerous goods ‘typeCode’

The new field, dangerous goods ‘typeCode’ enables shippers to share additional text type information along with the dangerous goods name.

Further, the Message Implementation Guideline (IFTMIN D96A) has been updated as the dangerous goods text type mapping (referring to new field ‘typeCode’ to EDI format) was enhanced with two new text types: DEL and SPH.

The mapping now distinguishes between dangerous goods names and special information, enabling carriers to accurately identify different text types. This prevents confusion when multiple dangerous goods text elements use the same code, improving data clarity for carrier systems.

New collaboration options for carriers on ocean transport: Message Implementation Guides released

There are three new implementation guides to support carriers in collaborating via EDI message:

  • ANSI X12 303 for booking cancellation (ocean)
  • ANSI X12 310 for freight receipt and invoice (ocean)
  • ANSI X12 315 for status details (ocean)

New field in Dock Appointment Scheduling: ‘Additional driver phone number’

To improve on-site coordination, a new field ‘Additional Driver Phone Number’ is now available under 'Driver Details' in the ‘Self-Book Dock Appointments’, ‘Manage Dock Appointments’, and ‘Manage Gate Operations’ applications.

Where available, both driver and additional driver information flow from the ‘Freight Orders for Confirmation’ application into Dock Appointment Scheduling, reducing manual entry and ensuring contact accuracy.

Providing network capability and integration: out-of-the-box integration with SAP Logistics Management for tendering and subcontracting

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration now offers out-of-the-box connectivity with SAP Logistics Management as the backend for logistics planning.

Shippers can send freight requests for quotation and consignment orders from SAP Logistics Management to SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration for tendering and subcontracting. Carriers can process requests for quotation in the ‘Freight Requests for Quotations’ application and, once a quotation is accepted, receive consignment orders for confirmation in the ‘Freight Orders for Confirmation’ application, confirming or rejecting them like standard freight orders.

For more information, see

  • Connecting with SAP Logistics Management
  • Integration with SAP Logistics Management Requests for Quotation (RFQs) and Consignment Orders.

 

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

Extended mode coverage: Tracking enabled for rail mode of transport

Enhancements have been made across different solution capabilities to extend tracking to rail mode of transport.

Standard Point Location Code (SPLC) is now supported as an alternative location ID in the ‘Manage Locations’ application. You can maintain SPLC when creating a location, allowing events to align more reliably with location master data. This enhancement benefits shippers and solution owners operating in rail and North American geographies.

Further, the 'trackedObject' in the Provide API for Order Events (GTT) supports a new resource type, RAILCAR_ID.

The’ Track Shipments’ application displays railcar ID in both the ‘Tracked Objects’ and ‘Tracking Units’ tables and the SPLC surfaces in the ‘Events Table’ and ‘Event Details’, allowing the system to derive full location information from master data and improving the accuracy of rail movement tracking for data contributors and solution owners.

Code list documentation has been updated with this tracked object type.

Additionally, the Location object in the same API supports SPLC to pinpoint where rail events occur.

Container tracking becomes easier: tracking is now also possible on container item level (via container ID)

The standard extractor now supports shipment tracking where container IDs are maintained on individual container items in freight bookings rather than as separate container units. With this model, tracking units are generated per container item and sent to logistics service providers. Any events reported on these tracking units are reflected in the corresponding freight unit and freight booking, maintaining end-to-end visibility.

For more information on the configuration, see Scenario 5: Generate Tracking Units based on Container Item

New collaboration option for ocean transports: Message Implementation Guide released for ANSI X12 315 Status Details (Ocean)

The “How to Integrate with SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace (ANSI X12)” guide now includes information about ANSI X12 315 (ocean status details).

Get ready for Joule and SAP generative AI use cases: Existing customers need to migrate authentication from XSUAA to IAS to be able to consume new capabilities

Identity Authentication and security Authentication is moving from SAP Authorization and Trust Management (XSUAA) to SAP Cloud Identity services – Identity Authentication service (IAS), SAP’s strategic solution for enterprise-grade identity and access management. This migration enables access to new capabilities such as Joule, SAP’s generative AI copilot. Existing customers should follow the guidance in “Migration of Authentication from XSUAA to IAS.”

New customers who onboard after the 2603 release will have IAS authentication enabled automatically during onboarding.

 

--------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2603. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2603 release can be found under the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

For further questions and feedback related to the blog, please check the Q&A area and feel free to post your questions in the link with the tag SAP Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: Re: SAP LGM problem with mobile applications
entry.content: Thank you for providing these details. I want to avoid that this is just a follow up issue now. So, can you please remove the local data of the app and try onboarding again. You can do this by a long press on the app icon, then select App info -> Storage & cache -> Clear storage. Then please start the app again and let us know if you still have this error. If yes, we would need a support user to check your tenant in detail.

entry.title: How to implement Draft-like staging or caching for Custom Entities with large datasets?
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community experts,

I am currently working with a Custom Entity where the data is retrieved through complex custom logic (custom handler). When dealing with large datasets and implementing pagination ($top/$skip), I’ve noticed a performance issue: the custom logic triggers repeatedly for every page request.

Is there a way to implement a Draft Mode or a similar staging/caching mechanism for Custom Entities?

My goal is to:

  1. Execute the full custom logic only once.

  2. Temporarily persist or cache the result set (similar to how Draft tables work).

  3. Allow subsequent pagination requests to fetch data directly from this "draft" or cache instead of re-running the entire logic.

Are there any best practices or built-in features in CAP/RAP to handle this scenario effectively?

Thanks in advance for your help!

entry.title: How do you restrict access to air freight related information in SAP (LBA compliance)?
entry.content:
Description:
We are currently dealing with a compliance requirement driven by an audit from the German Federal Aviation Authority (LBA).

The requirement is to ensure that only authorized users can access air freight related information in SAP. At the same time, users may still need access to the underlying documents, just without visibility of certain sensitive information.

We are interested in how other companies handle this in practice.

Discussion points:

  • How do you handle sensitive air freight related information within standard SAP transactions (for example display or change transactions)?

  • How do you manage and control the “known consignor” status within your processes and systems?

  • How do you ensure that only relevant users can see or work with this type of information?

  • What challenges did you face during audits or implementation?

  • What worked well, and what would you do differently today?

Goal:
We are mainly looking for an exchange of experiences and real-world approaches on how to handle LBA driven requirements around air freight information in SAP.

entry.title: Yes, SAP, actually language independent texts in a text view is supported.
entry.content:
Introduction

Hello, and welcome to yet another entry in my ongoing hit series (which I really should come up with a name for at some point), where I  try to close the gab between the old BW and Datasphere, or just try to help me fellow developers get to grips with life in Datasphere. 

Today, I'll use an edge-case to teach you some fundamental tricks that you can use in Datasphere.
I'll show you how to implement language-independent texts in a Text view, even though SAP does not want me to. 
See note 3221466 for reference.

A bit of background

So, if you've been playing around with SAP Datasphere , you'll have come across the framework of creating Dimension views and Text views, and linking them together through a Text-Association, which enables reporting on both IDs and Descriptions, in multiple languages. 

This method of creating a data model should be very familiar to people who've done CDS development, where you do the exact same thing. 
For BW developers, it may seem a bit odd, but BW does the exact same thing when you enable texts for an InfoObject. It creates a separate text table, regardless of whether or not you specify language-dependence or not. 

The problem

In Datasphere however, if you create a text view and you do not specify a language column, you'll get an error. 

In note 3221466, SAP specifies that this is the intended behavior. 
If you want a text without a language, that is, language independent texts, you have to add that column directly to the dimension, like below:

Wait, what's the problem?

Yes, so you may (correctly) raise an eyebrow and ask why on earth I keep getting upset over little things like this. 

Similar to my other blog post about translation texts, it may seem like I'm overreacting slightly. But this problem, just like the other one, has been a solved problem for decades in SAP software. 

So, dear reader, It bothers me to no end, because of three things:

  1. When working with CDS views, you absolutely can omit a language column in a text entity, and it works just fine. 
  2. What if you suddenly have to support multiple languages? Now you have to go through every single dimension and create new views, new associations and so on, rather than just being allowed to build it 'correctly' in the first place. 

But, the third and most painful, is that there is a very easy way to fix it. 

So, what can we do?

Before I go into the solution, I would like to take a moment out to complain a bit about the design of the Datasphere, that grates a bit on me. 

The problem deserves a longer blog at some point, but the short version is: Datasphere (or HANA Cloud, which Datasphere runs on) can do a lot more than the Datasphere documentation lets on.
At best I would say that it's though to write proper documentation, so "easter eggs" are to be expected. But at worst, I would say the following is going to be as much a surprise to the Datasphere developers as it is to you. 

Let's hope that's not the case. That would be laughable. 

The solution to our problem a little thing called sessions variables, which are some metadata values to your session.

You may actually have hit upon the SESSION_CONTEXT function when creating a view:

If you go to the official documentation you'll be served a rather cryptic message:

The <session_variable> can either be predefined or user-defined. Predefined session variables that can be set by the client are APPLICATION, APPLICATIONUSER, and TRACEPROFILE.

Which may give you the impression that those are the three options. But that is a lie. A big fat, stupid lie. 

If you have access to a database user (follow the steps in this blog post), you can 

select * from SYS.M_SESSION_CONTEXT	where CONNECTION_ID = CURRENT_CONNECTION;

You'll get something like this:

Which is the session variables for the HANA Explorer session.
But you can do something funny:

select distinct "KEY" from SYS.M_SESSION_CONTEXT;

Which will give you all the possible session variables, across all users. Here is a subset:

KEY
APPLICATION
APPLICATIONSTACK
APPLICATIONUSER
APPLICATIONVERSION
DRIVERVERSION
LOCALE
LOCALE_SAP
PROTOCOL_VERSION
SAP_PASSPORT
SPATIAL_OUTPUT_REPRESENTATION
XS_APPLICATIONUSER
XS_CLIENT_ID
XS_CUSTOM1
XS_CUSTOM2
XS_CUSTOM3
XS_CUSTOM4
XS_CUSTOM5
XS_DISPLAYNAME
XS_EMAIL
XS_FAMILYNAME

Which means, in your SQL console, you can try something like:

select *, SESSION_CONTEXT('LOCALE') from SYS.M_SESSION_CONTEXT where connection_id = CURRENT_CONNECTION;

And you'll get something like this:

Back in Datasphere, you can create an SQL view, you can use the "CURRECT_CONNECTION" function to get the session ID. This session is different than the HANA Explorer above:

"But Christian, do I have to use an SQL view? Can't I use a graphical view?"  You may reasonable ask.

No. When you create a graphical view, some validation checks against a set og legal statements. 
In an SQL view, you can use almost all the HANA SQL functions. A lot of fun stuff in there, like JSON-query.

"Okay... Why? That seems incredibly arbitrary"  You ask. Again reasonably.

And you know what - stop asking so many questions, reader! I'm having a meltdown here. Why does it always have to be about you?

Anyway.

I've provided some sample values for a session when you press "Data Preview" in a view.

KEYVALUE
APPLICATIONdragonet
APPLICATIONUSER<some value>
XS_APPLICATIONUSER<some value>
DRIVERVERSION02.27.23
PROTOCOL_VERSION4.1 (6,  10)
SAP_PASSPORT<some value>
XS_CLIENT_IDsb-approuter-sac-saceu20!<value>
XS_CUSTOM1[]
XS_CUSTOM2[]
XS_CUSTOM3[]
XS_CUSTOM4[]
XS_CUSTOM5[]
XS_DISPLAYNAME["Christian Gravesen"]
XS_EMAIL<some value>
XS_FAMILYNAME["Gravesen"]
XS_FAMILY_NAME<some value>
XS_FUNCTIONALAREA[]
XS_GIVENNAME["Christian"]
XS_GIVEN_NAME<some value>
XS_GROUPS["sac"]
XS_ISS<some value>
XS_PREFERREDLANGUAGE[]
XS_USER_NAME<some value>
XS_ZID<some value>

The XS_ variables are all related to your SAML / Identity provider properties. 

Notice something? The "LOCALE" and "LOCALE_SAP" is gone. 
But does that mean you can't use 'SESSION_CONTEXT('LOCALE')' in Datasphere views?

No! You absolutely can, even though it isn't in the list. Look!!!

And the output will be the value not match you chosen language - obviously. 
But! If you use that view in an analytical model (or a SAC story, or through the Excel plugin), it will match your "Data Access Language". 

"Well Christian, how the hell was I supposed to know that?"  You ask. 

You weren't! You have to be a bleeding idiot, or just clinically insane, to keep banging your head against the wall like I've done to figure these things out. 
But that's why you have me to do that. Lucky you. 

The actual solution now, promise.

Okay, so at this point, you might have already guessed the solution. 

You can create your Dimension view and your text view. If you don't have a "language" column yet, you can add a calculated column, 

left(SESSION_CONTEXT('LOCALE'),5)

and your language value will always match whatever the user is requesting. 
Note: The semantics for the language column is String(5), and can contain one to five characters. Everything form the old one-letter SAP locales (E for English, D for German, etc.), up to the modern ISO 3166 language / country formats (en_UK for English, en_US for Simplified English, etc.)

And that's it! Solved! 
You can create your text views for all your dimensions, whether or not they have a language column (yet), build your dimensions, build your models, and everything will work just perfect. 

"But Christian, why do I have to do that? Couldn't SAP do that for a text view in some runtime magic, if I don't define a language column?" 

Yes. That's how CDS views work. It's really that simple. 
But, you may ask, why the hell does Datasphere not work that way? Surely there is a complex, logical, highly technical reason?

And yes, dear reader. If we give people the benefit of the doubt, sure - there may be good reasons. 
But alas, dear reader, my guess is no. I'm beginning to suspect that whoever is developing Datasphere has never stopped to ask:

"Wait, this dosen't work. Maybe, maybe in our 50 years of software development, someone else already figured this out? Maybe I'll ask around the office". 

Either that, or not a single senior developer is on the same planet as the development team. 
But that would be absolutely insane. So surely that is not the case. 

And listen - I know we can't expect all the same functionality the old BW systems offered. That thing was developed over 20 years - I totally accept that. A gab in functionality - fine. 

But these are not specific problems - they are general problems across SAP software that was already solved countless times. Maybe, maybe we should expect that at least those problems are not reintroduced. 

Anyway, that's the post. 

I still like Datasphere, by the way. It's pretty good, all things considered.

entry.title: Re: Convert incoming time stamp from UTC to unix?
entry.content:
To get the correct UNIX timestamp, you need to round the current timestamp (var LV_CURRENT_TS) to the lowest integer.

Modify the code by inserting this statement instead of "lv_ts_packed = lv_current_ts.":

lv_ts_packed = round( val = lv_current_ts dec = 0 mode = cl_abap_math=>round_floor ).

entry.title: Best Practice for Exporting Large Data Volumes (>1 Million Records) in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

I am currently facing a limitation when trying to export large volumes of data from SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition).

In my case, the dataset exceeds 1 million records, and standard approaches such as:

Export to Spreadsheet (including background export from Fiori apps)

Custom CDS Views with analytical query export

are either failing, timing out, or not returning the full dataset.

I understand that there are system limitations when handling such large extractions, and I would like to align with SAP best practices.

Could you please advise on the recommended approach for extracting large datasets (1M+ records) from S/4HANA Cloud?

Specifically:

What is the best practice for large-scale data extraction in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?

Is it recommended to use OData APIs with pagination, and if so, what are the optimal limits per batch?

Are there any standard tools/apps (e.g., Data Export, SAP Analytics Cloud, or BTP services) that are designed for this purpose?

What are the performance and system limitations we should consider?

Is there any SAP-recommended architecture for recurring large data extraction (e.g., integration with data warehouse or external tools)?

Any guidance, references, or SAP notes would be highly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Best regards,
Ali

entry.title: Mail Sender Adapter For Microsoft 365
entry.content:
If you need to create a mail sender adapter for a mail address which is based on Microsoft 365, the step by step explanation is below.

First you need to understand that microsoft 365 use their authorization method as Oauth2 Authorization Code, so for you to define that you first need to ask the mail owner/admin to share the following informations  with you:

Mail Address, Password, client id, client secret, auth endpoint, token endpoint.

In addition to that in microsoft azure portal below parameters should be given

https://outlook.office.com/IMAP.AccessAsUser.All

https://outlook.office.com/SMTP.Send

Here is a screen shot of azure portal.

So after that you need to define this oauth2 authorization code in security material with the credentials you get.

Here is a screenshot of it.

 

When you deploy this, you will see it as unauthorized just like below.

It is not problem at all, you should click on three dot and click on authorize afterwards. It will open the mail login page in a new tab.

You can simply put e mail adress and password credentials and login, this will send an approval to the portal and you should ask them to approve. 

After that status of oauth2 authorization code will convert to deployed it means you can go to next step which is creating the sender mail adapter for getting the mails.

After that step you can basically go to your flow and create a mail sender adapter.

You should note that microsoft 365 use IMAPS so you should choose IMAPS.

Host and Port informations are as follows outlook.office365.com:993

In the processing tab you basically define what want to do with the e-mail, which folder you check and etc.

And finally there is a scheduler tab that you define pulling interval.

This way you finish your work with mail side of the flow. You can map the data inside that mail, data store send it to some other mail or you can use it as you need.

entry.title: Re: How to enforce a Maximum Stock Level per MRP Area (IT...
entry.content:
Thank you, Lakshmipathi — this is exactly the kind of clarification we needed.
Your summary aligns with our understanding: in standard S/4HANA, MRP will always try to cover demand and the Maximum Stock Level field doesn’t act as a physical cap.

We’ll now evaluate implementing a custom control using a Z-table and BAdI PPH_SUPPLY_DEMAND_LIST, as you suggested, to stop proposal generation once the defined threshold per MRP area is reached. We’ll also review the option of moving this constraint logic into EWM capacity checks, depending on functional scope.

Thanks again for confirming there’s no standard “hard stop” in MRP — this helps us shape our next steps internally.

entry.title: Can SAP DOCUMENT AI make automatic template selection?
entry.content:
This question was actually also asked back in 2022, but SAP Document AI has been updated 50-100 times since, so maybe a solution exist today.
We use Document AI basic edition.
We use SAP_purchaseOrder_schema or a extended copy of the same. We load purchase orders from different customers with different layouts and we see a lot of problems with the OCR not working in a consistent way. We hope we can fix some of these problems with templates. We would probably need templates for most of our customers POs.
We don't have a secure way to automatically determine which template to use before we load the PDFs.
Currently we can make a manual process where a user must open the pdf to see which customer it comes from and then manually load the pdf with the correct schema and template. This manual load process does not fulfill our goal for an automatic process.

The best solution will of course be if the OCR could be better so we wouldn't need a template, but a solution from the drawer with second best options could be if SAP Document AI automatic could detected the correct template from the templates related to the chosen schema.

Here is a a couple of examples of problems we hope to solve with templates. To be honest I don't think SAP Document AI performs very well in these cases:

1) A customer has more lines in the PDF for each item. The second line for an item has a scheduling line item no and SAP document AI reads this as a new item instead of just information for the previous item:

2) With line items that has identical layouts SAP Document AI suddenly swich from identifying a "Supplier material no" to instead identify a "Customer material no":

3) On page 1 SAP Document AI does not identify the "Supplier material no", but on page 2 it does.

Page 1: 

Page 2:

 

Best regards
Thomas Madsen Nielsen

entry.title: Re: Convert incoming time stamp from UTC to unix?
entry.content: To get the correct UNIX timestamp, you need to round the current timestamp to the smallest integer. Change the code to "lv_ts_packed = round( val = lv_current_ts dec = 0 mode = cl_abap_math=>round_floor )" on line 9 instead of "lv_ts_packed = lv_current_ts."

entry.title: Re: UK Plastic Tax Refunds
entry.content:
Only GI_ST is actually used.

It is used to transfer the refundable and non-refundable amount from one plant to another plant.

The statistical balancing approach works on plant and packaging element level.

If you produce in one plant, then do GI_ST to another plant and then export from the second plant, you want to have the refund.

However, the second plant may have its own inventory of sourced or produced packaging, so the mixture of the refundable / non-refundable inventories should be updated.

GR_ST is not really used for calculations.

Customers can still replicate these material documents for completeness and checks.

This is documented in the "Calculating Tax Relief for Exported Plastic Packaging" chapter in the report documentation

entry.title: Re: SAP RDP Integration Content for Packaging Fee Replica...
entry.content:
The mapping for Spain Plastic Tax will not work for UK Plastic Tax.

The API returns the relevant "expand" attributes only if the report configuration is for Spain Plastic Tax.

However, from legal perspective there are no specific requirements for printing the UK Plastic Tax in the invoice.

Hence the mapping for EPR should be sufficient, as it provides the fee per product (in this case the UK Plastic Tax fee).

If the customer would like something more, then a Customer Influence request should be created

entry.title: Portugal: Securing PDF Invoices with Qualified Signatures
entry.content:
Understanding the Legal Framework

The foundation for this change is Decreto-Lei n.º 28/2019, which regulates the processing and archiving of tax-relevant documents. While this law established the technical requirements for electronic invoices, a transitional provision allowed PDF invoices to be accepted as electronic invoices without a QES. This grace period is set to end on December 31, 2026, making the qualified signature mandatory from the start of 2027. All these requirements operate under the umbrella of the EU’s eIDAS Regulation, which standardizes electronic identification and trust services across the European Union.

What is a Qualified Electronic Signature?

A Qualified Electronic Signature (QES) is the highest standard of electronic signature under the EU’s eIDAS regulation. It is legally equivalent to a handwritten signature and is issued by a Qualified Trust Service Provider (QTSP). A QES guarantees a document’s origin and integrity, making any subsequent tampering detectable. You can find an official list of providers on the eIDAS Dashboard.

The Digital Signature Process Flow

Integrating a QES into your invoicing process typically follows a clear, automated workflow:

Invoice Generation: Your SAP system creates the invoice and generates a standard PDF file using its output management framework.

Secure Transmission: Middleware or a connector sends this PDF to your chosen QTSP’s signing service via a secure API call.

Signature Application: The QTSP service applies the Qualified Electronic Signature and a qualified timestamp to the document.

Return to SAP: The newly signed, legally compliant PDF is sent back to your SAP system.

Storage and Delivery: SAP archives the signed PDF for legal retention and delivers it to your customer through the designated output channel (e.g., email).

Achieving Compliance with SAP

SAP does not provide an end-to-end solution, instead provides specific guidance to facilitate this integration, which varies depending on your environment:

1. For On-Premise and Private Cloud Solutions: SAP advocates using an external signing service. Technical guidance is available for both Finance (FI) and Sales & Distribution (SD) invoices.

For FI invoices, refer to SAP Note 3680811 and the consulting note 3681477.

For SD invoices, refer to SAP Note 3682704.

For more information check the SAP online help.

2. For Public Cloud Solutions: In S/4HANA Cloud, digital signatures are managed through output control settings and integration with certified partner solutions. For a detailed walkthrough, review the SAP Help Portal documentation: Setting Up Output Management - Digital Signature.

Your Action Plan

With the deadline approaching, it is vital to act now:

Assess Your Systems: Identify all processes that generate PDF invoices.

Select a Provider: Choose a Qualified Trust Service Provider that aligns with your business needs.

Plan Your Integration: Design the technical workflow between SAP and the signing service using the resources noted above.

Test and Validate: Thoroughly test the end-to-end process to ensure the signatures are valid and meet all legal requirements.

By taking these steps, you can ensure a smooth transition and maintain full compliance for your invoicing operations in Portugal

 

Hope you find this information relevant.

Let me know in your comments.

Cheers, Elsa

entry.title: Depersonalization in Test Data Refresh – effects on Procurement Teams
entry.content:
During the performance of a Test Data Refresh (TDR), SAP offers three different levels of depersonalization:

Source: Depersonalization Feature | SAP Help Portal

 

In this blog, I want to highlight the effects of the choice of depersonalization mode on Procurement Teams.

 

Background Info:

In the App Manage Teams and Responsibilities – Procurement you can manage teams, responsibility definitions, and team member functions. You can use responsibility definitions and member functions to determine members (recipients) that are responsible for workflows, business scenarios, and situation handling. See Manage Teams and Responsibilities | SAP Help Portal for more info.

 

Effects on Procurement Teams:

When performing a TDR, a customer will have to decide on one of the three depersonalization mode. So how does this decision affect Procurement Teams?

 

An improvement request to prevent the necessity of changes when choosing Depersonalization On or Depersonalization Level 1 has been raised and is currently being investigated by SAP: Improvement Request 351502 - Retention of team members after Test Data Refresh.

So, in the future, there might be changes in this behaviour. But for now, from a procurement perspective it is necessary to pay close attention to the depersonalization mode.

 

Summary:

  • When choosing Depersonalization On or Depersonalization Level 1 the team owners and team members will be changed during TDR and need to manually be changed again afterwards.
  • Choosing Depersonalization On or Depersonalization Level 1 poses the risk of sending approval tasks to wrong employees.
  • When working with Depersonalization On or Depersonalization Level 1 it helps to turn off all workflows before performing a TDR.

 

Relevant sources to bookmark to find out about any updates:

  • 3070606 - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Test Data Refresh (TDR) - Information Note - SAP for Me
  • Test Data Refresh | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Webinar: Global HR, Local Compliance - Unlocking Localization in SAP SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Global HR leaders face a constant tension between standardization and local compliance. In this webinar, we examine how SAP SuccessFactors localization helps resolve that challenge - enabling consistent global HR processes while meeting country-specific regulatory requirements.

Discover how localized country versions support payroll, time management, and core HR with capabilities designed for both global oversight and local accuracy. Learn how SAP delivers more than 800 legal updates each year, helping organizations stay current as regulations evolve.

The session concludes with a live Q&A, offering the opportunity to discuss real-world localization considerations and compliance scenarios.
